items.title: Doctors Now Eligible To Claim Relocation Allowance For Permanent Posts
items.summary: Dr Dzul says medical officers are now eligible to claim for transfers for permanent positions, starting last June 22. But in the first batch receiving this new benefit, half of those posted to Sabah (19 of 39 doctors) didn't report for duty on June 29. The post Doctors Now Eligible To Claim Relocation Allowance For Permanent Posts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: How Singapore Can Effectively And Efficiently Co-Fund Private IVF Treatment — Dr Alexis Heng Boon Chin
items.summary: Singapore's existential demographic challenge requires bold, innovative action, and helping more couples access IVF quickly is a necessary step. But public funds must be spent on evidence-based medicine, not corporate upselling. The post How Singapore Can Effectively And Efficiently Co-Fund Private IVF Treatment — Dr Alexis Heng Boon Chin appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Permanent Appointments Alone Won’t Solve Doctor Retention In Sabah — MMA
items.summary: Permanency alone may not be sufficient to address the challenge of attracting and retaining doctors in Sabah and Sarawak. More attention should be given to measures that improve long-term retention, including greater flexibility in inter-state transfers. The post Permanent Appointments Alone Won’t Solve Doctor Retention In Sabah — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Lindung 24/7 Must Remain Mandatory For Everyone’s Benefit
items.summary: Lindung 24/7 must remain mandatory because social insurance only works when everyone, both rich and poor, contributes. Otherwise, it's unsustainable with a small risk pool; contribution rates may spike and claims get denied, like private health insurance. The post Lindung 24/7 Must Remain Mandatory For Everyone’s Benefit appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Poultry Producer SLH Expanding Despite Cost Pressures, Geopolitical Uncertainty
items.summary: SLH, a major Malaysian poultry producer, is pressing ahead with expansion, automation, and closed-house upgrades, despite rising feed costs and projected two years of market volatility. Smaller farmers are expected to face the greatest pressure. The post Poultry Producer SLH Expanding Despite Cost Pressures, Geopolitical Uncertainty appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: When The Pendulum Swings Too Far — Dr Ng Kuo Chern
items.summary: There is a profound difference between removing waste and reducing care to the lowest reimbursable denominator. If private health care is forced to use only what is cheapest, most standardised, and easiest to justify to payors, its role begins to change. The post When The Pendulum Swings Too Far — Dr Ng Kuo Chern appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Healthy Ageing Begins In Childhood
items.summary: Healthy ageing is not something we begin preparing for after retirement. It is built across the life course — from the womb, to the classroom, to the workplace, to the community, and ultimately into older age — one healthy decision at a time. The post Healthy Ageing Begins In Childhood appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Prescribing Authority Requires Medical Training, Clinical Accountability — PMPS
items.summary: PMPS opposes expanding prescribing authority through micro-credentialing alone. Prescribing follows diagnosis; they cannot safely be separated. Adopting one component of the UK's independent prescribing model without supporting infrastructure is risky. The post Prescribing Authority Requires Medical Training, Clinical Accountability — PMPS appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Don’t Forget The Invisible Child: Congenital Heart Disease And The Cost Of Being Overlooked — Hanis Syahira Hamdan
items.summary: No parent should have to crowdfund their child's surgery. When they do, it is not a story about community generosity, it is a story about a system that failed to plan. The post Don’t Forget The Invisible Child: Congenital Heart Disease And The Cost Of Being Overlooked — Hanis Syahira Hamdan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Doctor Shortage Now A National Pattern Beyond Departments — Specialist
items.summary: Doctor shortages are no longer about one department complaining; it's now a national pattern. Doctors leave when the system takes everything and gives back slogans. They leave when “service to nation” becomes a polite phrase for exploitation. The post Doctor Shortage Now A National Pattern Beyond Departments — Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .
